intro: Lastreopsis vieillardii, also known as Vieillard's fern or lastreopsis fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastreopsis vieillardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
